title: Acaena pallida
intro: Acaena pallida, also known as the hairy sheep's bur or woolly silver pine, is a perennial plant species belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to soothe digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Acaena pallida include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Acaena pallida is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea.
taxonomy: Acaena pallida is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is a type of silver grass or woolly head. The genus Acaena is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as the bluebells, woolly heads, or silver grasses. Acaena pallida is classified as a member of the genus Acaena and the family Rosaceae, which includes other plants such as roses and strawberries. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species in the Rosaceae family.
